source_file: AI Powered Legal Contract Risk Assessment (1).pdf

clauses.clause_text: This will cover: ● Risk Assessment ● Clause Extraction ● Obligation Tracking ● Compliance Checking ● Contract Comparison ● Negotiation Insights ● RAG-based Legal Q&A ● Explainability ● Architecture + Deployment Design Assumptions: ● Students know Python (intermediate) ● Allowed: LangChain, Ollama, open-source SLMs ● Python-only frontend + backend (Streamlit/FastAPI allowed) ● Weekly 1-hour demo ● Teams choose their own industry ● No actual deployment, but full architecture plan required 8-Week Capsule Plan Project: AI-Powered Legal Contract Intelligence System Week Focus Area Concepts Covered Technical Implementation Weekly Deliverable Demo Expectations Wee Problem Framing & Contract lifecycle, industry-specific contracts, risk types, Define industry (FinTech, Healthcare, SaaS, Real Estate, etc.), collect 10–20 sample contracts Problem Statement + Use Case Explain industry pain points k 1 Industry Selection legal NLP basics (public/redacted), define value proposition Document + Dataset & system vision Week 2 Contract Structure Understanding Legal clause taxonomy, NER in legal docs, document parsing Build contract parser (PDF → text), chunking strategy, clause segmentation, basic metadata extraction Working parser + structured JSON output Live parsing of contract into structured format Week 3 LLM Integration (Ollama + LangChain) Prompt engineering, SLM vs LLM behavior, structured outputs Integrate Ollama model, build clause extractor using prompt templates, output structured JSON Clause Extraction Module Live demo extracting clauses automatically Week 4 Risk & Obligation Intelligence Risk taxonomy design, obligation detection, rule-based + LLM hybrid logic Implement: Risk classification, Obligation extraction, Simple risk scoring algorithm Risk & Obligation Analyzer Show risk heatmap & obligation summary Week 5 RAG-based Contract Q&A & Compliance Embeddings, vector DB, Retrieval Augmented Generation Build RAG pipeline using contract corpus

entities.text: Legal Q&A ● Explainability ● Architecture + Deployment Design Assumptions

clauses.clause_text: allow natural language queries like: “What are termination penalties?” Contract Q&A module Live Q&A over uploaded contract Week 6 Advanced Intelligence Features Contract comparison, deviation detection, explainability, hallucination control Implement: Compare 2 contracts

add explainability layer (why risk assigned) Contract Comparator + Explainable Risk Engine Demo comparison of vendor vs standard template Week 7 Application Integration (Frontend + Backend) API design, modular architecture, logging, evaluation Build full Python app (Streamlit or FastAPI)

add logging & basic evaluation metrics Fully working integrated system End-to-end demo: Upload → Analyze → Report Week 8 Architecture & Deployment Planning System design, scalability, security, model hosting, compliance (GDPR, etc.) Design production-ready architecture (on-prem vs cloud), CI/CD plan, model serving plan, cost estimation Architecture Document + Deployment Plan + Final Presentation Final system demo + Architecture walkthrough Suggested Technical Stack Layer Technology LLM Ollama (Mistral / LLaMA / Phi / Gemma SLM) Framework LangChain Embeddings Ollama embeddings or SentenceTransformers Vector DB FAISS / Chroma Backend FastAPI Frontend Streamlit (recommended) Storage JSON / SQLite Logging Python logging module Suggested Repository Structure contract-ai/ │ ├── data/ ├── models/ ├── ingestion/ │ ├── parser.py │ ├── chunking.py │ ├── intelligence/ │ ├── clause_extractor.py │ ├── risk_engine.py │ ├── obligation_detector.py │ ├── comparator.py │ ├── rag/ │ ├── embedder.py │ ├── retriever.py │ ├── app/ │ ├── api.py │ ├── ui.py │ ├── evaluation/ ├── architecture/ └── main.py Industry Examples Students Can Choose ● Healthcare Provider Agreements ● SaaS Vendor Contracts ● Insurance Policies ● Real Estate Lease Agreements ● Supply Chain Agreements ● Employment Contracts ● Banking Loan Agreements Final Week Architecture Requirements They must design: ● Microservice architecture diagram ● Model serving layer ● RAG pipeline diagram ● Security & access control ● Data privacy compliance plan ● Cost estimation ● Monitoring strategy ● Human-in-the-loop workflow (No actual deployment required.)
